commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf(cli,code): reduce LAN grace timer, add push-based health SSE

- endpoints.ts: DEFAULT_PRIMARY_GRACE_MS 125→75ms (LAN-optimised Happy
  Eyeballs; primary is always <10ms on local network so 50ms of extra
  wait was pure latency overhead)
- http-server.ts: add /v3/health/stream SSE endpoint — emits full health
  payload immediately on connect, then 25s heartbeat; protected by same
  Bearer/query-token auth as /v3/health
- useConnectionHealth.ts: subscribe via EventSource to /v3/health/stream
  for instant connected/disconnected state changes (latencyMs measurement
  stays in the existing 30s poll loop)
- http-server.test.ts: assert /v3/health/stream rejects unauthenticated
  requests with 401

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add missing v1.9.0 changes (tasks #86-#88)

- Enhanced Helm test with metrics-check container (#86)
- emptyDir /tmp volume in DaemonSet, SECURITY.md updates (#87)
- Spring context tests for conditional beans (#88)

These changes were implemented locally but not included in PR #40.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: LLM truncation handling, dialogue splitting, attribution improvements

- Fix truncation detection: json.JSONDecodeError now raises TruncationError
  to trigger chapter splitting instead of wasting retries
- Cap MAX_RESPONSE_TOKENS at 16384 (was unlimited, caused 1.5hr hangs)
- Split dialogue/narration in parser: "Hello," said X, "bye." becomes
  3 segments (dialogue/narration/dialogue) for proper multi-voice TTS
- Full-chapter attribution prompt: LLM sees entire chapter for turn-taking
  instead of 2-segment context window
- Inline $ref in JSON schemas so Qwen can see enum values directly
- Print LLM errors to console (logging had no handler, errors were silent)
- Remove relationships field (50% of characters.json, never used downstream)
- Add TODO.md for voice_baseline + emotions TTS integration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extract functions exceeding 30-line ESLint max-lines-per-function limit
across 13 discord-bot source files. All 52 source-file violations resolved
by extracting helpers, splitting large methods, and decomposing complex flows.
No test files modified; all 1282 discord-bot tests pass.

Break down all 37 max-lines-per-function violations across 7 event
source files into sub-30-line methods and standalone helpers. No
behavioral changes — all 501 event tests pass, zero ESLint warnings
remain in events source files.

sentry (partial), steam (partial), system (partial).

fix: use content-based ingredient/step detection in MasterCook parser

Instead of relying on the dashed separator line (which may be missing
in some exports), detect ingredients vs steps by content: short lines
are ingredients, long paragraphs or lines starting with cooking verbs
are steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docker): run apk upgrade as root in nginx-unprivileged stage

nginx-unprivileged runs as UID 101 by default; apk needs root to write
to /var/log/apk/. Add USER root before the upgrade and restore USER 101
after so the container still runs as non-root at runtime.

commit.message:
fix(trust-eval): use LLM evaluation when user has a stored API key

Previously LLM trust evaluation only activated with USE_LLM_TRUST_EVALUATION=true
env var, so users who stored their own API key still got heuristic scores (73.0,
identical across messages, 60% confidence).

- conversation.routes: trigger LLM eval when the user has any active API key,
  not just when the server env flag is set
- llm-trust-evaluator: pass userId to llmService.generate() so it resolves
  the user's stored provider key (same pattern used for chat completions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: wmcaGetAccountIndexPwd returns const char* pointer, not buffer write

The DLL function returns a pointer to the encrypted password string,
not an int status code. Fix the typedef signature and read from the
returned pointer instead of passing a buffer.

commit.message:
perf: greedy + local search refinement for large order sets

Replace naive greedy fallback (>25 items) with a 3-phase algorithm:
1. Greedy largest-first baseline (O(n log n))
2. Swap refinement — binary search for better-fitting swaps (O(k log n))
3. Add pass — fill remaining gap with small items

Handles 100K-1M+ candidates in O(n log n) total while finding near-optimal
subsets. Example: budget=100, orders=[51,50,49,1] — greedy picks [51,49]=100
but naive would pick [51] then skip 50, skip 49 (51+49=100 > 100? no, 100=100
so actually ok here). Real benefit is with many similarly-sized orders where
greedy gets stuck early.

commit.message:
fix: PostgreSQL compat bugs in upload pipeline + loadtest fixes

Three server-side pgcompat bugs that broke the upload→index pipeline:
- `i32` literals for boolean columns (`is_zip`, `privileged`) fail
  tokio_postgres type checks against `bigint` columns — use `1i64`
- Ambiguous column refs in ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET for
  `project_corpus_stats` — qualify with table name
- `length()` returns Postgres INT4 but was deserialized as i64 —
  cast to `::bigint`

Also improves `internal()` error helper to log full anyhow chain
with `{e:#}` and `{e:?}` for easier debugging.

Loadtest fixes:
- Read API token from file (auth endpoint requires auth)
- Pure TypeScript ZIP builder (no system `zip` dependency)
- Correct search endpoint `/api/search` (was `/api/documents`)

Co-Authored-By: Sasha Duke <sashadanielduke@gmail.com>

  - Test 21: sidebar source system expands on first click
  - Test 22: sidebar second click collapses expanded system

- e2e/tests/auth.spec.ts:

    (undefined does not override the global storageState in Playwright)

commit.message:
Strip over-engineered P1/P2 features per MAGI 3:0 ruling

MAGI over-engineering audit (3:0 Conditional Approval) identified that
~60% of v3 additions were speculative infrastructure with zero user demand.

Removed (YAGNI + governance violations):
- Phase 4.5 Decision Log from SKILL.md (Bash not in allowed-tools)
- Phase 0 Topic Complexity Classification (adds friction, no evidence)
- Phase 5 "Generate action items" option (no Phase 6 exists)
- scripts/magi-ci.sh (speculative, fragile claude -p regex)
- scripts/magi-stats.sh (dead dependency on removed decision log)
- references/action-generation.md (no caller, no user demand)
- docs/MAGI_v3_ROADMAP.md (planning artifact, preserved in issue #19)

Retained (genuine improvements):
- validate-output.sh + 8 golden test fixtures (8/8 passing)
- extraction-fallback.md (documents undefined algorithm)
- $ARGUMENTS sanitization (<user_topic> wrapping)
- Config path validation (directory traversal prevention)
- magi-quick skill (kept for independent evaluation)

SKILL.md: 356 → 331 lines (66%). All governance checks pass.

Closes #28

- Always include is_active check

commit.message:
Fix adapter output: prefix import Mathlib and truncate at first example

The Goedel adapter outputs proofs without imports and generates many
example variations after the main theorem. This fix:
- Prefixes 'import Mathlib' in the prompt so the model continues from it
- Tells the model to output ONE declaration only
- Truncates extracted code at the first 'example' or second declaration
- Reduces retry context to save tokens for 8K context models
